summary.intent: Add a Clear button to the session header in the chat UI, as requested in GitHub issue #671.
summary.outcome: Fixed by removing `showClear=${false}` from the `headerModalTopMount` in `page-chat.js`, allowing the prop to default to `true` and making the Clear button visible in the 'More controls' modal for the main session.

repo: SessionHeader is mounted three times in page-chat.js: toolbar, modal body (sessionControlsSection), and modal top (headerModalTopMount).
The modal body mount (sessionControlsSection) is hidden when on the main session, so it cannot surface the Clear button there.
The Clear button visibility is doubly gated: the `showClear` prop AND an `isMain` guard in session-header.js ensure it only renders for the main session.

code.finding: `showClear` defaults to `true` in the SessionHeader component.
Guard `showClear && isMain` controls whether the Clear button renders — only shows for the main session.
Both visible SessionHeader mounts (toolbar and modal top) had `showClear=${false}` explicitly set, suppressing the Clear button entirely.

workflow: Biome is used for JS linting/formatting; passes cleanly after the fix.

friction: The root cause required reading multiple files and mounts to understand why a defaulted-true prop was never showing — the suppression was spread across two call sites.
open_items: The toolbar mount still has `showClear=${false}` — if a Clear button in the toolbar is ever desired, that would need a separate change.
The PR was requested to be created but the transcript ends at commit/push; PR creation outcome is not confirmed.
